</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: N-sulfonylate 4-bromo-1H-pyrrole-2-carbaldehyde with pyridine-3-sulfonyl chloride (base, e.g. Et3N/DMAP) to give 4-bromo-2-formyl-1-(pyridin-3-ylsulfonyl)-1H-pyrrole | Step 2: Suzuki coupling of this bromopyrrole aldehyde with 3-cyano-4-fluorophenylboronic acid (Pd catalyst, base) to form 4-(3-cyano-4-fluorophenyl)-2-formyl-1-(pyridin-3-ylsulfonyl)-1H-pyrrole | Step 3: Reductive amination of the aldehyde with tert-butyl methylcarbamate (BocNHMe) using NaBH(OAc)3 to install the CH2N(Me)Boc side chain, giving CN(Cc1cc(-c2cc(C#N)ccc2F)n(S(=O)(=O)c2cccnc2)c1)C(=O)OC(C)(C)C.

  }
}
</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: O-alkylate 2,5-dichloro-3-hydroxypyridine with 2-bromoacetaldehyde diethyl acetal, then acid-promoted cyclization/deacetalization to build the fused core 4,7-dichlorofuro[2,3-b]pyridine | Step 2: Regioselective bromination at the activated 2-position of the furopyridine (e.g., NBS) to give 2-bromo-4,7-dichlorofuro[2,3-b]pyridine | Step 3: Pd-catalyzed sp2/sp3 Suzuki coupling of 2-bromo-4,7-dichlorofuro[2,3-b]pyridine with N-Boc-(S)-3-pyrrolidinyl boronic ester to furnish 2-[(S)-1-Boc-pyrrolidin-3-yl]-4,7-dichlorofuro[2,3-b]pyridine | Step 4: Boc deprotection (TFA or HCl/dioxane) to give the target Clc1cc2oc([C@H]3CCCN3)cc2nc1Cl.

student_answer: Step 1: Protect trans-4-(aminomethyl)cyclohexan-1-ol with CbzCl/base to give benzyl ((4-hydroxycyclohexyl)methyl)carbamate. | Step 2: Convert the C1 alcohol into a leaving group (e.g., PBr3 or MsCl then LiBr) to obtain benzyl ((4-bromocyclohexyl)methyl)carbamate. | Step 3: Prepare/use the fused NH-lactam heterocycle Cc1onc2c1c(=O)[nH]c1cnccc21, deprotonate it (NaH or Cs2CO3, DMF), and N-alkylate with benzyl ((4-bromocyclohexyl)methyl)carbamate to give the target Cc1onc2c1c(=O)n(C1CCCC(CNC(=O)OCc3ccccc3)C1)c1cnccc21.

student_answer: Step 1: Start from 2-aminobenzonitrile (anthranilonitrile); treat with hydrazine hydrate to convert the nitrile into the amidrazone, giving 2-aminobenzamidrazone (o-H2N–C6H4–C(=NNH2)NH2). | Step 2: Diazotize the ortho aniline with NaNO2/HCl at 0–5 °C to form the o-diazonium amidrazone intermediate. | Step 3: Warm to promote intramolecular cyclization of the amidrazone onto the diazonium center, then rearomatization/workup furnishes Nc1nnc2ccccc2n1 (4-aminobenzotriazine target).

</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: N-alkylate 4-bromo-1H-pyrazole with 4-bromo-1-methylpiperidine to give 4-bromo-1-(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)-1H-pyrazole. | Step 2: Miyaura borylation of that bromopyrazole (B2pin2, Pd) to the corresponding pyrazolyl-Bpin intermediate. | Step 3: Suzuki couple the pyrazolyl-Bpin with 6-bromo-4-chloro-1-methyl-pyrazolo[3,4-b]pyridine to obtain 4-chloro-6-(1-(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-1-methyl-pyrazolo[3,4-b]pyridine (the heterobiaryl chloride). | Step 4: In parallel, prepare the target-configured trans-4-methyl-3-hydroxypyrrolidin-2-one (the C[C@@H](O)[C@H]1CNC(=O)C1 fragment) from L-threonine by esterification/protection, intramolecular lactamization, then deprotection. | Step 5: Generate the alkoxide from that enantiopure hydroxyl lactam and displace the remaining 4-chloro substituent on the heterobiaryl chloride by SNAr to furnish C[C@@H](Oc1nc(-c2cnn(C3CCN(C)CC3)c2)cc2nn(C)cc12)[C@H]1CNC(=O)C1.
